data_source: NYC DOB permit issuance data, NB job type
methodology_notes: Year-over-year percentage change in new building permits issued. Excludes demolition and alteration permits. 2020 excluded from percentile calculations due to COVID outlier (-42%).
Year-over-year percentage change in residential certificates of occupancy issued. Completions lag permits by 18-36 months. Typical annual completions: 20,000-35,000 units.

Year-over-year percentage change in citywide median asking rent for market-rate apartments. Median market rent range during period: $2,100-$3,400. Excludes 2020 COVID trough (-8.5%) from percentile calcs.

data_source: NYC RGB Orders / DHCR registration data
methodology_notes: Effective annual increase in median registered stabilized rent, combining RGB guideline increases with turnover-driven rent resets. Median stabilized rent range: $1,100-$1,650. Lower bound reflects 2016-2017 and 2020-2021 freezes.

Year-over-year percentage change in total HPD violations issued (Classes A, B, and C combined). Annual violations typically 500,000-650,000 citywide. Spikes correlate with enhanced enforcement campaigns and winter severity.

Year-over-year percentage change in median price per unit for multifamily (5+ unit) building sales. Post-HSTPA (2019) saw significant value declines in stabilized-heavy portfolios (-10% to -25%). Typical range: $150,000-$450,000 per unit depending on borough and regulation status.

Citywide net rental vacancy rate from triennial NYCHVS. Below 5% triggers automatic continuation of rent stabilization under state law. Range: 1.41% (2023, historic low) to 4.54% (2021, COVID-era). Pre-2020 average was roughly 3.2%.
